narration: Comment out the else and finally blocks to focus on try-except.
Demonstrate a file not found error by attempting to open 'test file.txt' without the underscore.
Explain that traceback errors provide useful information but should not be displayed to end users.
Move the error-throwing code into the try block and handle the exception in the except block.
Print a custom error message in the except block instead of the default traceback.
Explain that a generic except block catches all exceptions, including unexpected ones.
Demonstrate that a generic except block catches both file not found errors and name errors.
Change the except block to specifically catch FileNotFoundError.
Explain that specific exceptions should be caught before generic ones to avoid catching unexpected errors.
Add a second except block to catch general exceptions.
Use the 'as' keyword to assign the caught exception to a variable for printing.
Print the exception object instead of a custom message in the general except block.
Uncomment the else block to demonstrate its functionality.
Explain that the else block executes only if no exception is raised in the try block.
Demonstrate the else block by printing file contents and closing the file when no exception occurs.
Explain that code in the else block should not be placed in the try block to ensure specific error handling.
Uncomment the finally block to demonstrate its functionality.
Explain that the finally block executes regardless of whether an exception is raised.
Demonstrate the finally block by printing a message after the else block executes.
Demonstrate that the finally block executes even when an exception is raised in the try block.
Explain that the finally block is useful for releasing resources regardless of success or failure.
Raise a custom exception manually using the 'raise' keyword.
Demonstrate that manually raised exceptions are caught by the except block.
Comment out the manual exception raise to show normal file handling behavior.
